rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Acco Engineered Systems, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court's own motion to October 7, 2014. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY YAEGER SR et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is overruled. Based on the matters of which this court takes judicial notice, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' action is time-barred. While the amendment that substituted defendant for a Doe occurred after the relevant statute of limitations had run, defendant failed to demonstrate that such amendment does not relate back to the filing of the initial wrongful death action. Defendant failed to show that at that time plaintiffs knew of defendant's identity and the facts rendering it liable. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits 1, 2 and 3 and denied as to Exhibits 4, 5 and 6. However, even if the Court granted defendant's request for judicial notice in its entirety, the conclusion would be the same. Defendant must answer within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GREGG RANERI VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 27 and 28 and overruled as to the remainder of his declaration. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BROSNAN VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fdcc California, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 2, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint is denied. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages and defendant did not demonstrate that such a claim is barred against a dissolved corporation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
